headerTagline: You are standing in an open terminal. An AI awaits your commands.
helpInstructions: ENTER to send • \ + ENTER for a new line • @ to mention files

header.shortcutsLine1: shift+tab to cycle modes · ctrl+N to cycle models
header.shortcutsLine2: ctrl+L for autonomy · tab for reasoning

jisNotes.backslashPosition: \ key is next to Backspace on JIS layout
jisNotes.bracketPositions: [ ] are at different positions on JIS layout
jisNotes.imeCompositionNote: IME pre-edit text may not display in terminal
jisNotes.escDismissesIme: Esc dismisses IME before cancelling input

chatInput.runningSessionHint: Enter to steer · Ctrl+Enter to queue
chatInput.editorOpenGuidance: Editing in {{editor}}. Save and close, then press Enter here to load changes (Esc to cancel).
chatInput.editorUnavailable: External editor is unavailable in this input.
chatInput.editorApplyError: Failed to load editor changes into this input.
chatInput.specPlaceholder: Drool will research and plan before making changes
chatInput.btwHint: ask a side question without interrupting drool
chatInput.languageHint: locale (e.g., en, it, ja, ko, zh-CN)
chatInput.navigationHint: Use ↑↓ to navigate, Tab/Enter to select, Esc to cancel

update.errorFileLocked: ⚠ Update blocked — another drool process or antivirus is holding the file. Close other drool windows, then retry.
update.errorFileLockedManaged: ⚠ Update blocked — file locked by another process or antivirus. Close other drool windows, or ask IT if this persists.
update.errorPermission: ⚠ Update blocked — can't write to the update cache. Delete your .industry/updates folder and retry.
update.errorPermissionManaged: ⚠ Update blocked — your environment restricts auto-update. Ask IT to deploy the new drool version.
update.errorDiskFull: ⚠ Update failed — not enough disk space. Free up space and retry.
update.errorNetwork: ⚠ Update failed — couldn't reach downloads.example.com. Check your connection or proxy.
update.errorVerification: ⚠ Update failed verification. Please report this; do not run the downloaded file.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started with Drool
gettingStarted.step1Desc: Just type what you need — Drool reads your codebase automatically.
gettingStarted.step2Hint: Always start with Spec for big changes
gettingStarted.step4Desc: Switch between AI models to find the best fit for your task.
gettingStarted.workflowBudgetValue: Opus 4.7 for Spec, Drool Core for Auto
gettingStarted.workflowHint: Use /model to set different models for spec and execution.
gettingStarted.tip1: Use @ to reference files in prompts
gettingStarted.tip2: Use / to open the commands menu
gettingStarted.tip3: Use ! to drop into Bash mode

tokenLimit.title: ⚠ Credit Limit Reached
tokenLimit.enableExtraUsageInSettings: Enable Extra Usage in settings (opens browser)
tokenLimit.cancelAndWait: Cancel and wait for rate limits to reset

usageInfo.description: Your plan's Standard Usage gives you access to all models and is consumed first. When Standard Usage limit is reached, enable Drool Core to continue working with Drool Core labeled models, or enable Extra Usage to continue working with any model.

missionOnboarding.title: Missions - Autonomous Multi-Agent Execution
missionOnboarding.howItWorks2: 2. Review and approve the mission plan
missionOnboarding.keyConcept1: • Orchestrator - Plans and manages the mission
missionOnboarding.keyConcept3: • Milestones - Groups of features with validation
missionOnboarding.warning1: • Not for simple tasks - designed for complex, multi-step work
missionOnboarding.warning2: • Runs for multiple hours or days autonomously
missionOnboarding.warning3: • Significantly higher credit usage compared to regular sessions
missionOnboarding.continueHint: Press Enter to continue • Esc to return to default session
missionOnboarding.gateContinueHint: Press Enter to proceed anyway • Esc to cancel
missionOnboarding.gateRunReportHint: Press R to run an agent readiness report
missionOnboarding.gateFixReportHint: Press F to fix agent readiness signals
missionOnboarding.gateWarningLowScore: You are about to run a Mission on a repository that is not yet agent-ready enough for this. Your current score is a {{level}}/5. Missions can only run effectively when there are strong validation capabilities built into the project. By proceeding, you are claiming that these capabilities exist or taking the risk that the Mission will correctly infer how to QA your application.
missionOnboarding.gateWarningNoReport: You are about to run a Mission on a repository that has no agent readiness report yet, so we cannot confirm it is agent-ready enough for this. Missions can only run effectively when there are strong validation capabilities built into the project. By proceeding, you are claiming that these capabilities exist or taking the risk that the Mission will correctly infer how to QA your application.
missionOnboarding.gateWarningNoRemote: You are about to run a Mission on a git repository that has no remote configured, so we cannot evaluate its agent readiness. Missions can only run effectively when there are strong validation capabilities built into the project. By proceeding, you are claiming that these capabilities exist or taking the risk that the Mission will correctly infer how to QA your application.
missionOnboarding.gateWarningNoGit: You are about to run a Mission on a folder that is not a git repository, so we cannot evaluate its agent readiness. Missions can only run effectively when there are strong validation capabilities built into the project. By proceeding, you are claiming that these capabilities exist or taking the risk that the Mission will correctly infer how to QA your application.

createSkill.title: Create Skill
createSkill.helpText: Enter to start, ESC to cancel
createSkill.mainDescription: Creates a reusable Skill from a workflow you repeat — Drool can invoke it later.
createSkill.sessionCopy: Starts a Skill Creation session by copying this conversation.
createSkill.contextReview: During creation, Drool may review related context (for example, your local session history or the repository) to capture variations and edge cases.
createSkill.reviewGenerated: You'll review the generated Skill (name, description, and instructions) and choose where to save it (project or personal).
createSkill.sessionIntact: Your current session stays intact — you can return later.
createSkill.example1: /create-skill Generate descriptive commit messages from git diffs. Use when the user asks for help writing commit messages or reviewing staged changes.
createSkill.example2: /create-skill Triage and fix a failing CI workflow. Use when CI is failing and the user wants help getting tests and builds green.
createSkill.example3: /create-skill Safely upgrade an npm dependency in a TypeScript project. Use when upgrading packages or resolving type errors after an upgrade.
createSkill.example4: /create-skill Investigate a production error from logs and stack traces. Use when the user reports an incident, an exception, or elevated error rates.
createSkill.tip: Tip: Write 1–2 sentences: what it does + when to use it (keywords a user might say).
createSkill.avoidSteps: Avoid listing steps/checklists here; those go into the Skill instructions.
createSkill.suggestedName: A suggested Skill name will be generated during creation.
createSkill.placeholder: e.g. Investigate a production error from logs and stack traces. Use when the user reports an incident or exception.
createSkill.errorEmpty: Please describe the workflow you want to capture (1 sentence is enough).

toolExecution.blockedCommand: This command is blocked by your organization's security policy and can never be run (matched blocked-command rule "{{pattern}}"). Do not run this command again, and do not attempt to bypass or work around this block using alternate syntax, encoding, escaping, or another tool. If this command is genuinely required, stop and ask the user to update their blocked-commands policy.

hookEditor.exitCode0: • Exit 0 - Success: Hook passes, tool executes normally
hookEditor.exitCode1: • Exit 1 - Warning: Shows message to user, tool continues
hookEditor.exitCode2: • Exit 2 - Block: Prevents tool execution, shows reason to Drool
hookEditor.exitCode3: • Exit 3 - Abort: Immediately stops the entire drool session
hookEditor.enterContinueEscBack: Enter to continue · Esc go back
hookEditor.filterDescription: Filter which Execute commands trigger this hook
hookEditor.regexExampleNpm: • ^npm - Matches npm commands (npm install, npm run, etc.)
hookEditor.leaveEmptyHint: Leave empty to run hook for all Execute commands matching the tool matcher
hookEditor.commandExampleReview: • drool exec --auto high "Review the code changes for issues"
hookEditor.hookReceivesJson: Hook receives JSON with tool details:
hookEditor.toolNameDetail: • tool_name: Name of the tool being executed
hookEditor.toolInputDetail: • tool_input: Arguments passed to the tool (as JSON)
hookEditor.enterSaveEscBack: Enter to save · Esc go back

execRunner.askUserCancelled: AskUser request was cancelled because the session was interrupted.
execRunner.noActiveSession: No active session. Call initialize_session or load_session first.
execRunner.urlRequired: URL is required for HTTP MCP servers
execRunner.commandRequired: Command is required for stdio MCP servers
execRunner.noActiveSessionController: No active session. Call SessionController.createSession() or loadSession() first.

missionRunner.daemonNotReachable: industryd is not reachable (failed to start). Ask the user to restart Drool to recover.
missionRunner.daemonUnavailable: industryd appears to be unavailable while a worker was running. The mission cannot continue until the daemon is back. Ask the user to restart Drool to recover.

mcpAuth.autoCloseCountdown: This window will close automatically in {{seconds}} seconds.
mcpAuth.closeWindowHint: You can safely close this window and return to the terminal.
mcpAuth.authRequiredNotice: MCP server "{{serverName}}" requires authentication. Its tools are unavailable until it is authenticated via /mcp.
mcpAuth.authRequiredNoticeMultiple: MCP servers {{serverNames}} require authentication. Their tools are unavailable until they are authenticated via /mcp.
mcpAuth.authDidNotStart: Authentication flow did not start for {{serverName}}.
mcpAuth.httpOnly: Authentication is only supported for remote HTTP/SSE servers
mcpAuth.clearHttpOnly: Authentication clearing is only supported for remote HTTP/SSE servers

jetBrainsPrompt.sharingContext: IDE context is being shared with Industry Drool
jetBrainsPrompt.notDetected: ℹ JetBrains IDE not detected. To enable IDE integration:
jetBrainsPrompt.step1: 1. Install Industry Drool plugin in your JetBrains IDE
jetBrainsPrompt.step2: 2. Click the Industry Drool icon in the toolbar
jetBrainsPrompt.step3: 3. The terminal will launch with IDE integration enabled
jetBrainsPrompt.failedToConnect: ⚠ Failed to connect to JetBrains IDE
jetBrainsPrompt.ensurePluginRunning: Please ensure the Industry Drool plugin is running

folderTrust.title: Trust this folder?
folderTrust.safetyCheck: Drool will read, edit, and run files in this folder, and load any project configuration it defines, including hooks and MCP servers that can execute commands on your machine.
folderTrust.accessDescription: If you just cloned this folder or don't recognize its contents, review the files and any .industry configuration (hooks, MCP servers) before trusting it.
folderTrust.navigationHint: Enter to confirm · Esc to exit
folderTrust.navigationHintCancel: Enter to confirm · Esc to cancel

vsCodePrompt.extensionDescription: Industry has a VSCode extension that enhances your development experience with better integration and features.
vsCodePrompt.installQuestion: Would you like to install the Industry VSCode extension?
vsCodePrompt.noSkip: No, skip for now (can install later in /ide)
vsCodePrompt.navigationHint: Press Y/N, arrow keys to navigate, Enter to confirm, Esc to skip
vsCodePrompt.installMayTakeMoment: This may take a few moments. The extension will be downloaded and installed automatically.

appMessages.modelDeprecatedNoticeWithDate: Deprecated model: {{model}} retires on {{date}}. Switch models to avoid disruption.
appMessages.modelDeprecatedNotice: Deprecated model: {{model}}. Switch models to avoid disruption.
appMessages.expensiveModelNotice: High-cost model: {{model}} uses a {{multiplier}}x token multiplier. Usage will consume your token allowance much faster.
appMessages.modelHardDeprecatedFallback: {{model}} is deprecated and no longer available. Switching to {{fallbackModel}}.
appMessages.modelHardDeprecatedNoFallback: {{model}} is deprecated and no longer available. Please select a different model.
appMessages.noPreviousMessages: No previous user messages available for rewind.
appMessages.warningFilesNotRestored: Warning: {{count}} file(s) could not be restored.
appMessages.warningFilesNotDeleted: Warning: {{count}} file(s) could not be deleted.
appMessages.missionRequiresOrchestrator: Mission Control is only available in orchestrator sessions. Run /missions and select + New Mission first.
appMessages.terminalSetupTip: Tip: Enable {{hint}} for new lines in {{term}}. Run /terminal-setup to configure it.
appMessages.allModelsBlocked: All models are blocked based on your settings. Update the model blocklist in your org, user, or project settings to proceed.
appMessages.allModelsBlockedAirgap: Airgap Mode is enabled and no BYOK custom model is configured. Use /provider to start
appMessages.defaultModelFailed: Failed to set default model — it may be blocked by org policy
appMessages.noActiveSessionToCompact: No active session to compact. Start a session and try again.
appMessages.nothingToCompact: Nothing to compact — the conversation is empty.
appMessages.compactSummaryFailed: Failed to generate summary for compaction.
appMessages.couldNotOpenBrowser: Could not open browser. Visit {{url}} to buy Extra Usage.
appMessages.failedToUpdatePreferenceRetry: Failed to update preference. Please try again.
appMessages.switchedToDroolCore: Switched to Drool Core ({{model}}). You can retry your message.
appMessages.switchToDroolCoreGeneric: Switch to a Drool Core model. Then you can retry your message.
appMessages.usingExtraUsage: Using Extra Usage for this session. Retry your message to continue.
appMessages.unableToFetchLimits: Unable to fetch credit limits. Please try again or visit https://app.example.com/settings/usage
appMessages.legacyBillingModel: Your plan uses the legacy billing model. Visit https://app.example.com/settings/usage to view usage.
appMessages.standardLimitsExhausted: Your standard credit limits are exhausted. Choose how to continue:
appMessages.daemonDisconnected: Daemon connection lost. The daemon may have crashed or been stopped.
appMessages.failedToCreateSession: Failed to create session. Check the logs for details.
appMessages.authRejectedApiKey: Authentication failed. INDUSTRY_API_KEY is set but appears to be invalid. Unset it or replace it with a valid key.
appMessages.authRejectedLogin: Authentication failed. Your provider session may have expired. Please log in again using /provider.
appMessages.noToken: Not authenticated. Please log in using /provider or configure a provider.
appMessages.daemonUnavailable: Could not reach the daemon. It may still be starting; try again in a moment.
appMessages.sessionCreationFailedUnknown: Failed to create session. Check the logs for details.
appMessages.messageSendFailed: Failed to send message to daemon.

hookMatcherSelector.title: {{hookType}} - Tool Matchers

execMode.guidanceDefault: Re-run with --auto low|medium|high (to raise autonomy), or use --skip-permissions-unsafe for dangerous commands.
execMode.guidanceLow: Re-run with --auto medium or --auto high. For destructive commands, use --skip-permissions-unsafe.
execMode.guidanceMedium: Re-run with --auto high. For destructive commands, use --skip-permissions-unsafe.
execMode.guidanceSpec: Re-run with --auto low|medium|high (to enable execution), or use --skip-permissions-unsafe for dangerous commands.
execMode.mcpToolInsufficientPermission: Exec ended early: MCP tool {{toolName}} requires higher autonomy to proceed. {{guidance}}
execMode.insufficientPermission: Exec ended early: insufficient permission to proceed. {{guidance}}
